channel.title: Data Breach & Ransomware Aggregator
channel.description: Real-time data breach notifications from state registries, federal sources, and ransomware trackers

item.title: Pyramid Advisors Limited Partnership d/b/a Pyramid Global Hospitality
item.description:
Pyramid Advisors Limited Partnership d/b/a Pyramid Global Hospitality reported a state registry affecting - records in Boston, MA.
📊 Records Affected: - | 📍 Location: Boston, MA | 📰 Source: Maine AG | 🏷️ Type: State Registry

item.title: Miz Mooz, Inc
item.description:
Miz Mooz, Inc reported a state registry affecting - records in Brooklyn, NY.
📊 Records Affected: - | 📍 Location: Brooklyn, NY | 📰 Source: Maine AG | 🏷️ Type: State Registry

item.title: How ‘silent probing’ can make your security playbook a liability
item.description:
Silent probing uses AI to measure your team’s response patterns over time, building a behavioral profile that makes follow-on attacks harder to spot and easier to time. The post How ‘silent probing’ can make your security playbook a liability appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: The FBI’s cyber chief is using Winter SHIELD to accelerate China prep, threat intelligence sharing
item.description:
Brett Leatherman is running the bureau's most public cyber campaign yet, pushing basic security hygiene while quietly preparing industry for stepped-up Chinese threats. The post The FBI’s cyber chief is using Winter SHIELD to accelerate China prep, threat intelligence sharing appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Google addresses actively exploited Qualcomm zero-day in fresh batch of 129 Android vulnerabilities
item.description:
The company’s latest security update contains the highest number of Android vulnerabilities patched in a single month since April 2018. The post Google addresses actively exploited Qualcomm zero-day in fresh batch of 129 Android vulnerabilities appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Possible U.S.-developed exploits linked to first known ‘mass’ iOS attack
item.description:
Researchers traced the kit moving from a spyware vendor’s customer to Russian hackers to Chinese cybercriminals. The post Possible U.S.-developed exploits linked to first known ‘mass’ iOS attack appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Cisco SD-WAN Zero-Day Under Exploitation for 3 Years
item.description:
The maximum-severity vulnerability CVE-2026-20127 was exploited by an unknown but sophisticated threat actor who left very little evidence behind.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Marquis v. SonicWall Lawsuit Ups the Breach Blame Game
item.description:
When a company gets breached through a third-party security vendor, who should bear responsibility? For one FinTech company, the answer is the firewall provider.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Claude Code Security Shows Promise, Not Perfection
item.description:
Claude Code's introduction rippled across the stock market, but researchers and analysts say its impact was overstated, as they peel back the layers.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: The Case for Why Better Breach Transparency Matters
item.description:
It's become a standard practice for organizations to disclose the bare minimum about a data breach, or worse — not disclose the incident at all.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Life Mirrors Art: Ransomware Hits Hospitals on TV & IRL
item.description:
HBO's "The Pitt" is showing audiences what a real Mississippi healthcare system is going through this week, thanks to a ransomware attack.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Quantum-Resistant Data Diode Secures Sensitive Data on Edge Devices, Critical Systems
item.description:
Forward Edge-AI's new Isidore Quantum is a compact, low-power hardware device designed to defend sensitive operational technology endpoints against future quantum attacks.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Critical OpenClaw Vulnerability Exposes AI Agent Risks
item.description:
The now-patched flaw is the latest in a growing string of security issues associated with the viral AI tool, which has seen rapid adoption among developers.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: As War Continues, Pro-Iranian Actors Launch Barrage of Cyberattacks
item.description:
Iran and its supporters have taken to cyberspace to retaliate for US-Israeli military action, with an aim to cause economic and physical disruption.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Qualcomm Zero-Day Exploited in Targeted Android Attacks
item.description:
The exploitation activity against CVE-2026-21385, a high-severity memory corruption flaw, could be tied to commercial spyware or nation-state threat groups.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Dark Reading Confidential: This Threat Hunter Helped Cops Bust Up An African Cybercrime Syndicate
item.description:
Dark Reading Confidential Episode 15: Interpol relied on Will Thomas and team to help break up a sprawling cybercrime ring, leading to the arrest of 574 suspects, the recovery of more than $3 million, and the decryption of six malware variants. Here's his story.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Vulnerability in MS-Agent AI Framework Can Allow Full System Compromise
item.description:
Improper input sanitization in the framework can be exploited through the Shell tool, allowing attackers to modify system files and steal data. The post Vulnerability in MS-Agent AI Framework Can Allow Full System Compromise appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Iran Cyber Front: Hacktivist Activity Rises, but State-Sponsored Attacks Stay Low
item.description:
The cybersecurity industry is monitoring the landscape and says many of the big claims made by hacktivist groups remain unverified. The post Iran Cyber Front: Hacktivist Activity Rises, but State-Sponsored Attacks Stay Low appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Android Update Patches Exploited Qualcomm Zero-Day
item.description:
An integer overflow or wraparound in the Qualcomm graphics component, the bug leads to memory corruption. The post Android Update Patches Exploited Qualcomm Zero-Day appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: 1.2 Million Affected by University of Hawaii Cancer Center Data Breach
item.description:
Hackers stole names, Social Security numbers, driver’s license information, voter registration records, and health-related information. The post 1.2 Million Affected by University of Hawaii Cancer Center Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: New ‘AirSnitch’ Attack Shows Wi-Fi Client Isolation Could Be a False Sense of Security
item.description:
Researchers have uncovered a Wi-Fi vulnerability that allows nearby attackers to intercept sensitive data and execute machine-in-the-middle attacks against connected devices. The post New ‘AirSnitch’ Attack Shows Wi-Fi Client Isolation Could Be a False Sense of Security appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Honeywell, Researcher Clash Over Impact of Building Controller Vulnerability
item.description:
The researcher says he has identified thousands of internet-exposed IQ4 building management controllers. The post Honeywell, Researcher Clash Over Impact of Building Controller Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Fig Security Launches With $38 Million to Bolster SecOps Resilience
item.description:
The company was founded in March 2025 and it has now emerged from stealth mode. The post Fig Security Launches With $38 Million to Bolster SecOps Resilience appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Iranian Strikes on Amazon Data Centers Highlight Industry’s Vulnerability to Physical Disasters
item.description:
Two AWS data centers in the United Arab Emirates were “directly struck” and another facility in Bahrain was also damaged after a drone landed nearby. The post Iranian Strikes on Amazon Data Centers Highlight Industry’s Vulnerability to Physical Disasters appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Western allies form 6G security coalition amid tech rivalry with China
item.description:
The Global Coalition on Telecoms (GCOT) — comprising the United Kingdom, United States, Canada, Japan and Australia, with Sweden and Finland joining at the launch — unveiled voluntary security and resilience principles for the technology at the Mobile World Congress trade show in Barcelona.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: LexisNexis says hackers accessed legacy data in contained breach
item.description:
The breach emerged this week when a threat actor claimed they stole 2 GB worth of information from the company that included millions of records.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
item.description:
Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.description:
Verification alert Listings attributed to DRAGONFORCE have been reported as including unverified or fabricated victim...
🎭 Threat Actor: DRAGONFORCE | 📰 Source: Red Packet Security | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

item.title: January 2026 Healthcare Data Breach Report
item.description:
The HHS’ Office for Civil Rights (OCR) healthcare data breach portal shows a slight month-over-month decline in large healthcare data […] The post January 2026 Healthcare Data Breach Report appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Asheville Eye Associates Settles Lawsuit Stemming from DragonForce Ransomware Attack
item.description:
Asheville Eye Associates, an eye care provider serving patients in Western North Carolina, has agreed to settle class action litigation […] The post Asheville Eye Associates Settles Lawsuit Stemming from DragonForce Ransomware Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Trizetto Data Breach: PHI of 3.4 Million Individuals Exposed
item.description:
It has been more than four months since TriZetto Provider Solutions discovered unauthorized access to its IT environment, and it […] The post Trizetto Data Breach: PHI of 3.4 Million Individuals Exposed appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: University of Hawai’i Cancer Center: 1.15 Million Individuals Affected by 2025 Ransomware Attack
item.description:
The University of Hawai’i Cancer Center (UHCC) has confirmed that up to 1.15 million individuals may have been affected by […] The post University of Hawai’i Cancer Center: 1.15 Million Individuals Affected by 2025 Ransomware Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: QualDerm Partners Data Breach Affects More Than 3 Million Individuals
item.description:
In late February, The HIPAA Journal reported on a QualDerm Partners data breach, the scale of which was currently unknown, […] The post QualDerm Partners Data Breach Affects More Than 3 Million Individuals appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: FREE WEBINAR: Smarter HIPAA Incident Management
item.description:
Discover how to replace scattered HIPAA incident reports with a single, streamlined system that keeps you audit ready and in […] The post FREE WEBINAR: Smarter HIPAA Incident Management appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: A Comprehensive Guide to HIPAA Designated Record Sets
item.description:
This guide to HIPAA designated record sets is designed to reduce common misunderstandings about how individually identifiable non‑health information may […] The post A Comprehensive Guide to HIPAA Designated Record Sets appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Insight Hospital and Medical Center Announces Cyberattack & Data Breach
item.description:
Data breaches have been announced by Insight Hospital and Medical Center in Chicago and Community Health Action of Staten Island. […] The post Insight Hospital and Medical Center Announces Cyberattack & Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Alabama man pleads guilty to hacking, extorting hundreds of women
item.description:
A 22-year-old Alabama man pleaded guilty to extortion, cyberstalking, and computer fraud charges after hijacking the social media accounts of hundreds of young women (including minors). [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: CyberStrikeAI tool adopted by hackers for AI-powered attacks
item.description:
Researchers warn that a newly identified open-source AI security testing platform called CyberStrikeAI was used by the same threat actor behind a recent campaign that breached hundreds of Fortinet FortiGate firewalls. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Android gets patches for Qualcomm zero-day exploited in attacks
item.description:
Google has released security updates to patch 129 Android security vulnerabilities, including an actively exploited zero-day flaw in a Qualcomm display component. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: UH Cancer Center data breach affects nearly 1.2 million people
item.description:
The University of Hawaii confirmed that a ransomware gang stole the data of nearly 1.2 million individuals in August 2025 after breaching its Cancer Center's Epidemiology Division. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Star Citizen game dev discloses breach affecting user data
item.description:
Cloud Imperium Games (CIG), the game developer behind Star Citizen and Squadron 42, says attackers breached systems containing some users' personal information in January. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Compromised Site Management Panels are a Hot Item in Cybercrime Markets
item.description:
Compromised cPanel credentials are being sold in bulk across underground channels as plug-and-play phishing and scam infrastructure. Flare explains how analyzing 200,000 underground posts reveals a commoditized market for hacked site management panels. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: LexisNexis confirms data breach as hackers leak stolen files
item.description:
American data analytics company LexisNexis Legal & Professional has confirmed to BleepingComputer that hackers breached its servers and accessed some customer and business information. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Microsoft: Hackers abuse OAuth error flows to spread malware
item.description:
Hackers are abusing the legitimate OAuth redirection mechanism to bypass phishing protections in email and browsers to take users to malicious pages. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Paint maker giant AkzoNobel confirms cyberattack on U.S. site
item.description:
The multinational Dutch paint company AkzoNobel has confirmed to BleepingComputer that hackers breached the network of one of its U.S. sites. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: CISA flags VMware Aria Operations RCE flaw as exploited in attacks
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a VMware Aria Operations vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-22719 to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, flagging the flaw as exploited in attacks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: CISA Releases New Guidance on Assembling Multi-Disciplinary Insider Threat Management Teams
item.description:
Ashley Nyquist, Ashden Fein, Caleb Skeath, John Webster Leslie, Matthew Harden, Catherine McGrath, and Samar Amidi of Covington and Burling write: On January 28, 2026, the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) published a new resource on Assembling a Multi-Disciplinary Insider Threat Management Team.The guidance is intended to assist critical infrastructure stakeholders, which includes private... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Shutdown Stalls Compliance Plans for Cyber Breach Reporting Rule
item.description:
Cassandre Coyer reports: A partial government shutdown threatens to further derail a key federal cybersecurity agency’s incident reporting rule—and delay answers that companies need to comply. The Department of Homeland Security shutdown, now entering its third week, may push back the finish line for a Biden-era rule that would create stringent disclosure requirements for critical infrastructure entities after... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Hacktivists claim to have hacked Homeland Security to release ICE contract data
item.description:
Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai reports: A group of hacktivists calling themselves “Department of Peace” claimed to have hacked the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), leaking allegedly stolen documents online. On Sunday, the nonprofit transparency collective DDoSecrets published data relating to contracts between DHS, Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), and more than 6,000 companies, including defense contractors Anduril, L3Harris, Raytheon,... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Project Compass: first operational results against The Com network
item.description:
Europol reports: In its first year of operation, Project Compass has delivered concrete operational results against “The Com”, a decentralised extremist network targeting minors and vulnerable individuals both online and offline. Since January 2025, Project Compass has contributed to: 4 victims safeguarded 30 perpetrators arrested 62 identified and partially identified victims 179 identified and partially... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Wisconsin k-12 district hit by weeklong outage
item.description:
Dysruption Hub reports: A reported “cyber incident” left the Denmark School District in the Village of Denmark, Wisconsin, without internet access for five school days, forcing teachers and students to rely on paper-based workarounds, according to a local news report. But that “cyberincident” appears to be a cyberattack by INC Ransom, or so the gang... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Data from Insight Hospital and Medical Center Leaked on Dark Web
item.description:
On or about January 26, 2026, Insight Hospital and Medical Center (“Insight”) in Chicago issued a substitute notice. It states that in September 2025, Insight learned of unusual activity within its network. An investigation subsequently determined that an unauthorized individual accessed the network between August 22, 2025 and September 11, 2025. As of the date... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Evoke Wellness at Hilliard updates its breach notification
item.description:
On February 27, external counsel for OCAT, LLC dba Evoke Wellness at Hilliard (“Evoke”), submitted a breach notification to the Maine Attorney General’s Office. The sample notification letter submitted with it claims that the Ohio addiction treatment center learned of an incident on August 7, 2025: On August 7, 2025, OCAT became aware of unauthorized... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Congress finds data brokers cost consumers tens of billions of dollars
item.description:
A congressional investigation estimates broker breaches have cost consumers $20 billion in identity theft. Major brokers now promise to make it easier to opt out of their databases. By: Colin Lecher Breaches at data brokers have cost American consumers more than $20 billion, Congress’s Joint Economic Committee revealed Friday as part of an investigation triggered... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Israeli spies ‘hacked every traffic camera in Tehran to plot killing of Iran’s Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’
item.description:
Maira Butt reports: Israeli spies hacked nearly every traffic camera in Tehran for years in order to monitor the movements of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in an unprecedented intelligence-gathering campaign, according to a report. Officials surveilled highly trained and loyal security guards, bodyguards and drivers of senior Iranian officials to pick up on their “pattern of life”, the Financial Times reported. This real-time data, including from cameras focused... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: 1,700 Dutch police officers get reminder not to access files without legitimate purpose
item.description:
Dutch Police reports: Nearly 1,700 police officers will receive a letter in the coming period because they used police systems when there was likely no need to do so. These colleagues were looking for information about the violent death of 17-year-old Lisa from Abcoude. The letter is primarily intended to remind police officers of the... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Unknown
item.description:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: United Faith Ministries
